page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of system memory. Powered by an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, this instance allocates one physical core per vCPU without simultaneous multithreading. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1 instruction and data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard integer and multi-core processing, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 7217.84 and single-core scoring 1812.79. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in memory-bound and complex computational tasks. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is relatively low at 281.72 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write speeds reach 47.64 GB/sec and 28.32 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic operations and LLM inference tasks perform poorly, with the Passmark encryption test scoring 648.53 and small-model text generation limited to 34.59 tokens per second.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-4 offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads that do not require x86-specific instruction sets or high-performance vector extensions. The dedicated ARM cores provide predictable execution for multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and basic web serving. This instance is best utilized for ARM64 software compilation, lightweight database hosting, static web serving, and general-purpose application testing.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, powered by a 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. Operating with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and one thread per physical core, it lacks local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 7217.84, alongside average database operation performance. However, it exhibits weak results in memory bandwidth, cryptographic execution, and LLM inference. This instance provides a qualitatively cost-efficient platform for ARM64 development, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an ARM-based general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for lightweight database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an ARM-based instance with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, designed for general-purpose ARM64 workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring four dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and a balanced memory profile for standard utility workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
16 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support without SMT overhead
Suited for ARM64 software compilation, static web serving, and utility databases
